accuracy.note: IMPROVED vs Sprint 10 (0.9231 -> 1.000, +0.077). The Sprint 11 item #1 taxonomy refresh unlocked the column-name engine's ability to classify credential columns correctly — before the fix, expected label was CREDENTIAL which had no column_name_engine entry, so credential columns scored as FN even when blinded column names clearly identified them. After the fix, OPAQUE_SECRET matches the blinded column name signal and the column produces a clean TP.

UNCHANGED vs Sprint 10 (0.7742 -> 0.759, within averaging noise — TP/FP/FN counts identical). Sprint 10 handover's diagnosis that this regression was 'fake taxonomy drift' turns out to be only partly correct: Sprint 8's CREDENTIAL split WAS stale in the loader, but fixing it does not change the benchmark counts because the root cause is the secret scanner over-firing API_KEY + EMAIL on plaintext passwords / 6-digit PINs / UUID-ish tokens in col_7 (expected=OPAQUE_SECRET). Before fix: predicted=API_KEY vs expected=CREDENTIAL. After fix: predicted=API_KEY vs expected=OPAQUE_SECRET. Same FP+FN either way. Filed new P1 bug secret-scanner-over-fires-api-key-and-email-on-plaintext-password-pin-uuid-values-nemotron-col-7-surface as the real lever for recovery; owned by the parallel sprint11/scanner-tuning-batch session.

IMPROVED vs Sprint 10 (0.6111 -> 0.667, +0.056). The Sprint 10 0.6111 baseline was artificially deflated by the same stale-CREDENTIAL labeling. Taxonomy refresh correctly scores the formerly-invisible credential-column detections.

NEW baseline for Sprint 11. First-ever measurement for the Gretel-finance corpus (Sprint 10 ingested the loader but never wired the CLI flag). Item #2 (wire-gretel-finance-corpus-into-accuracy-benchmark-cli) closed this gap. Named-mode F1 of 0.917 is strong for a credentials-in-financial-prose corpus — the column-name engine carries most of the weight in named mode.

NEW baseline for Sprint 11. First-ever blind measurement for Gretel-finance. 0.800 is notably higher than Gretel-EN blind 0.667 and Nemotron blind 0.759 — Gretel-finance's long-form financial-document prose gives the regex engine richer context windows than the short isolated-token shapes in Nemotron/Gretel-EN. This validates the Sprint 10 hypothesis that Gretel-finance breaks the 'credentials-as-short-tokens' shortcut learning that M1 CV diagnostics flagged.

note: Sprint 11 items #1 (corpus loader taxonomy refresh) and #2 (wire gretel_finance CLI) executed. Item #1 scope EXPANDED during execution from Nemotron-only to all 3 loaders that emitted the stale CREDENTIAL label (Nemotron + Gretel-EN + _DETECT_SECRETS_TYPE_MAP). Net effect: 2 hidden wins from item #1 (Nemotron named +0.077 to perfect 1.000, Gretel-EN blind +0.056), 1 unchanged (Nemotron blind, blocked by independent scanner over-firing bug, filed as separate P1). Item #2 adds first-ever Gretel-finance blind+named baselines (0.800 / 0.917) — the CLI flag was never wired in Sprint 10 despite the loader landing. Sprint 10 handover's 'fake regression' diagnosis was only half right — taxonomy drift was real and worth fixing, but Nemotron blind F1 recovery requires the scanner-side fix. Pending Tier 2-3 items: corpus-loader-drift-lint, tighten-id_token-match-type, env-leak hygiene, document-all-engines, document-fail-fast-dispatch.
